title: Job Costing & Commission Analyst – Construction
description:
We are a growing residential roofing and exterior remodeling company based in the United States seeking a highly detail-oriented professional to support our accounting and operations team.



This role focuses on job costing data entry, financial analysis, and commission preparation. Accuracy, organization, and analytical thinking are essential. The person in this role will help ensure the integrity of our financial data and assist leadership in maintaining clear visibility into job profitability.



This position works directly with company leadership and plays an important role in maintaining accurate project financial records.



Core Responsibilities



• Enter and maintain job costing data from QuickBooks Online and Acculynx our internal systems

•Analyze estimate vs. actual job costs and identify discrepancies

• Assist with commission preparation 

• Review project financials to identify patterns, inconsistencies, or missing costs

• Maintain organized records in Google Sheets and/or Excel

• Assist with financial data reconciliation between systems

• Monitor job profitability and flag unusual margins or anomalies

• Support leadership with data analysis and reporting



Required Qualifications



We are looking for someone who takes pride in precision, organization, and analytical thinking.



Must have:



• Exceptional attention to detail

• Strong ability to recognize patterns, discrepancies, and errors in financial data

• Experience with QuickBooks Online

• Strong experience using Excel and Google Sheets

• Understanding of job costing concepts

• Construction industry knowledge (roofing and exterior remodeling strongly preferred)

• Experience with AccuLynx or similar construction CRM/project management software

• Strong written and spoken English communication skills

• Ability to understand American terminology and business communication

• Comfortable asking questions when something doesn’t look correct

• Strong
 organization and professional communication habits



Technical Requirements



• Reliable high-speed internet connection

• Quiet professional workspace

• Minimum two-monitor workstation required

• Ability to work across multiple systems simultaneously

• Comfortable switching between QuickBooks, AccuLynx, spreadsheets, and

• Availability to work during U.S. Central Time business hours



Personality Traits That Fit This Role



This role is best suited for someone who:



• Is extremely detail-oriented

• Naturally works in an organized and methodical way

• Enjoys investigating numbers and solving financial puzzles

• Can spot inconsistencies or errors in reports

• Is proactive about raising questions when something doesn’t make sense

• Maintains accuracy and focus while working independently



Preferred Experience



• Accounting, bookkeeping, or financial analysis background

• Experience with job costing in construction companies

• Commission calculation

• Data reconciliation between software systems



What Success Looks Like in This Role



• Accurate job cost tracking

• Clean and organized financial records

• Reliable commission calculations

• Early identification of financial discrepancies

• Improved visibility into job profitability



Application Instructions



To be considered, please follow these instructions carefully.



In the first line of your application, write the phrase:



“Job Costing Requires Precision.”



Applications that do not include this phrase will not be reviewed.



Please also include answers to the following questions:



Describe your experience with QuickBooks Online job costing.



Have you worked with construction companies or roofing contractors? Please explain.



What steps would you take if job costs did not match the estimate?



What Excel or Google Sheets functions do you regularly use?



Describe a time you identified a mistake or discrepancy in financial data.



Please also include:



• Your resume

• Your available working hours in U.S. time zones



If you enjoy working with numbers, spotting details others miss, and contributing to the financial clarity of a growing company, we look forward to reviewing your application.

title: Inside Sales Agent Real Estate/ Cold Caller / Full time and Part time
description:

Execute consistent follow-up via calls, text messages, and
s.

Calls made



Contacts made



Appointments set



Appointments showed



Maintain accountability for follow-up and conversion activities.

 and SMS nurture campaigns.

Appointment set vs showed





Conversion rates





Pipeline performance

Train agents and tea
mbers on proper CRM usage.

title: Real Estate Portfolio Finance Manager
description:
*KINDLY READ THE JOB DESCRIPTION & THE APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS CAREFULLY.*



Who We Are:



Tracing our history back to 1920, EJF Real Estate Services has been a family-owned and family-operated business for over 30 years, specializing in community association management and real estate sales across the Washington, DC metropolitan area. Today, we are the largest DC-based property management firm, proudly serving hundreds of clients throughout the DMV. Our extensive market knowledge, hands-on industry experience, and best-in-class customer service help communities thrive. 



What You'll Do:



As a Finance Manager, you’ll oversee the financial reporting and analysis for a portfolio of community associations. This is a client-facing, oversight role — not transactional bookkeeping. You’ll ensure accurate monthly financials, guide Boards of Directors in understanding their financial position, and partner with internal teams to resolve issues. This position is ideal for an experienced accounting professional who thrives in a dynamic, client-service environment.



Requirements:



Ensure monthly financial reports accurately reflect the association’s financial position.

Review general ledgers, statements of revenue/expenses, and balance sheets to resolve issues and correct errors.

Serve as the primary financial resource for both internal tea
mbers and external clients (board members and association representatives).

Oversee and ensure completion of administrative tasks, including tax payments, end-of-year tax returns, and government filings.

Support external audits for associations within the portfolio.

Review and monitor banking and investment activity.

Analyze and review loans, special assessments, and other financial obligations as required.

Provide long-term financial planning guidance for capital projects.

Attend weekly tea
etings, set goals, and complete assigned tasks on time.

Perform additional duties as assigned.



Qualifications:



Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field (or equivalent experience).

3+ years of accounting, financial experience in property management (HOA, condo, coop) is required.

Experience working with Vantaca property management software is required.

Strong knowledge of GAAP and financial reporting.

Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills.

Strong interpersonal and communication skills with the ability to present financial information clearly to non-financial stakeholders.

Must be able to analyze data issues and execute on solutions.

Proficiency in accounting software and Microsoft Excel.



Working Hours: 9:00 am – 5:00 pm EST; Monday – Friday (US time zones)



Benefits: HMO and PTO after 90 days.

title: Ecommerce Administrative Assistant (Operations + Notion + Shopify) — Full-Time
description:
We are a category-leading women’s eCommerce brand on a mission to create premium, affordable, and 100% waterproof jewelry that enhances our customers’ everyday lives. We’ve served over 1,000,000 customers worldwide in more than 145 countries!



We’re hiring one or more sharp, proactive Administrative Assistants to support operations and execution across the business.



This is not a “basic VA role.” If you need reminders, struggle with details, or prefer slow-paced work, this role will not be a fit.



We are looking for someone who is:



• fast, organized, and detail-obsessed

• proactive (you spot issues before being asked)

• fluent in Notion (databases + task systems + SOPs)

• fluent in Shopify (orders + products + inventory fields)

• able to work reliably with Dubai time (Gulf Standard Time, UTC+4)



Working Hours (Dubai Time Requirement)



This is a full-time role with consistent daily coverage. To be considered, you must have consistent availability in Dubai time (UTC+4). Required working hours: 8 am – 4 pm Dubai time (UTC+4), Monday–Friday. Please do not apply if you cannot reliably cover these hours.



What You’ll Own (Core Responsibilities)



1) Operations Execution + Coordination



• Maintain internal task systems in Notion (databases, assignees, due dates, SOPs)

• Support daily operations tracking (orders, stock, shipments, urgent issues)

• Create structure: checklists, templates, and systems to keep execution clean



2) Shopify Admin Support



• Process and monitor orders, returns, and exchanges

• Update product listings and key Shopify admin fields accurately

• Maintain operational accuracy across inventory / pricing / product details (basic Shopify fluency is required — this is not optional)



3) Admin & Team Support



• Scheduling, calendar coordination, meeting agendas, notes, follow-ups

• Document management, data entry, reporting, and structured file organization

• Ensure tasks don’t slip through cracks (high ownership required)

• You will be expected to spot problems, propose solutions, and keep execution moving without needing constant direction



Additional Support Areas (As Needed)



This is primarily an operations + admin role. If you have additional skill, you may also support:



• light marketing coordination (uploading, scheduling, simple assists)

• invoice tracking / basic bookkeeping support

• research projects (suppliers, competitor scans, simple analysis)



Requirements (Must-Haves)



• 2+ years of experience as an admin assistant, operations assistant, or eCommerce VA

• Strong Notion fluency (databases, templates, task systems, SOP organization)

• Strong Shopify fluency (orders, products, admin navigation, accuracy)

• Extremely strong attention to detail

• Excellent English communication (written + verbal)

• Able to work full-time and maintain Dubai time coverage



Bonus if you have:



• Richpanel or other helpdesk tools

• eCommerce fulfillment coordination experience

• spreadsheet reporting experience



If you’re a high performer, this role can become a long-term, career-defining growth path inside our company.



Competitive pay based on experience.



How to Apply



Please complete this application carefully (we will not review low-effort submissions):

title: Remote Cold Caller (Real Estate – Home Staging / Interior Design)
description:
I’m looking for a strong cold caller to reach out to real estate brokers in California and book qualified consults for my home staging and interior design business.



This is not a script-reading role. You will be speaking to busy brokers, handling objections in real time, and guiding the conversation toward booking a walk-and-talk consultation.



What you’ll be doing:

Calling real estate brokers daily

Getting past “not interested” and keeping the conversation going

Asking the right questions about their listings

Positioning the service clearly and confidently

Booking qualified consults on my calendar



You must be comfortable handling objections like:

“My client doesn’t want staging”

“We already have a stager”

“No budget”

“Not interested”



You are expected to stay in control of the call, respond naturally, and move the conversation forward.



Requirements:

Strong spoken English

Confident, calm, and persistent on the phone

Proven experience in cold calling and booking appointments

Able to think on your feet and not rely on scripts

Reliable internet and quiet calling environment



Performance:

This is a results-driven role. I track calls made, conversations, and consults booked.



To apply:

Send a short voice recording (1–2 minutes) where you respond to this objection:

“I’m not interested, my client doesn’t want staging.”

Then show how you would continue the conversation and move toward booking a call.



There will be a paid trial period for the right candidate.

* Leverage AI and innovative tools to improve Business Development processes

Enable the Managing Director to focus exclusively on:

* High-level sales conversations

* Enterprise deal structuring

* Strategic account development

* Own all research, tracking, documentation, and follow-up execution.



Core Responsibilities

1. Prospect Research & Target Development

Build and maintain target account lists (CPG brands, agencies, tech conference organizers, experiential marketers)

Research each account (retail footprint, event calendar, funding status, decision-makers)

Identify strategic expansion signals (new distribution, funding, product launches)

Prepare briefing documents before executive calls



2. CRM Management (HubSpot)

Ensure 100% CRM hygiene and compliance

Log meeting notes and summaries

Update deal stages accurately

Track follow-up dates and reminders

Maintain contact records and segmentation. No deal should move without documentation.



3. Outbound Support

)

Schedule meetings

Monitor dormant leads and trigger re-engagement. This is structured, not spam outreach.

s

title: Finance & Operations Assistant (QuickBooks & Google Sheets Expert)
description:
We are looking for a detail-oriented 

 Finance and Operations Assistant to handle the day-to-day bookkeeping and data tracking for a federal construction firm. This is not just a "data entry" role; we want a teammate who understands the "why" behind the numbers.

The primary focus is financial accuracy—managing QuickBooks, reconciling accounts, and maintaining complex Google Sheets for job costing. We are starting this as a **part-time position (approx. 20 hours/week)** with the goal of moving into a full-time role as you become an essential part of our operations.

   Compensation & Growth

 * **Starting Rate:** $7.00 – $10.00 USD per hour (depending on experience).

 * **Growth Potential:** We believe in rewarding high performance. If you are "killing it"—providing high value, catching errors, and helping the company run smoother—there is significant room for your salary and responsibilities to grow.

Key Responsibilities

 1. Finance & Bookkeeping (Core Focus)

 * **QuickBooks Management:** Import bank statements, categorize expenses for tax purposes, and perform weekly reconciliations.

 * **Job Costing:** Use receipts and digital data to create detailed expenditure reports for specific project items.

 * **Financial Tracking:** Update the Cashflow Statement, Profit & Loss (P&L), and Debt Schedule weekly.

 * **Spreadsheet Analysis:** Use Google Sheets to total weekly expenditures, compare them against monthly billings, and project expenses against cash on hand.

 * **Data Visualization:** Maintain simple graphs in Google Sheets showing expenditures versus cashflow.

 2. Payroll & Equipment Admin

 * **Payroll Support:** Review weekly employee hours for accuracy, import them into the payroll system (Square), and send them to the owner for final review.

 * **Payment Calendar:** Track equipment and rental payment dates; send out reminders at least one week in advance.

 * **Equipment Logs:** Update Google Sheets for equipment maintenance and damages.

 3. Operational Support & Coordination

 * **Daily Reporting:** Coordinate with the project Foreman to collect daily site photos and a brief summary of work accomplished.

 * **Weather & Team Sync:** Fill out daily weather reports and help set the agenda for weekly tea
etings.

 * **Team Collaboration:** We want a collaborator, not someone who works in total isolation. You will be expected to communicate openly, ask questions, and suggest improvements to our processes.

 Technical Requirements

 * **QuickBooks Expertise:** You must be comfortable reconciling statements and categorizing expenses by job.

 * **Google Sheets Advanced Skills:** You must be highly skilled with formulas, pivot tables, and creating professional-grade charts/graphs.

 * **Financial Literacy:** A strong understanding of P&L statements, job costing, and cashflow management.

 Qualifications

 * Experience in a finance-heavy VA role or a background in accounting/bookkeeping.

 * **Proactive Communication:** You don't wait to be told what to do; you see a deadline approaching and you hit it.

 * **Attention to Detail:** You take pride in "clean" books and error-free spreadsheets.

 * Excellent written English for reporting and team communication.

****Important Application Instruction

To ensure you have read this entire post and follow directions well, please include the word **"STEEL"** at the very beginning of your application subject line or message. Applications without this keyword will not be considered.***

title: Program Manager
description:
We are looking for a highly organized and people-focused Program Manager to support the day-to-day operations of one of our client communities. This role blends customer success, operations, and community engagement, making it ideal for someone who enjoys creating smooth client experiences while managing key backend logistics.



Key Responsibilities

-Act as a key point of contact for community members, offering timely support and consistent communication

-Track client needs, feedback, and overall satisfaction to improve retention and experience

-Scheduling & Coordination

-Schedule and manage program-related
s, including reminders, updates, and event notifications

-Coordinate and organize live events, group calls, and coaching sessions, ensuring all logistics are handled

Qualifications
